codeReview.summary: The implementation is solid and covers all six expectedBehavior items. The diff adds 198 lines to src/server.ts: CSS for .token-balance, two HTML balance divs (hidden by default), fetchTokenBalance with eth_getBalance/eth_call paths, 30-second balance cache, formatBalance with comma separators and 6-decimal limit, update triggers on wallet connect/disconnect, token selection, and chain change. Error handling silently hides failures as specified. The code follows existing patterns (void async calls, dataset.address, findTokenByAddress). Two non-blocking issues noted.

issues.description: NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS is set to 42 asterisks ('******************************************') instead of the expected native ETH sentinel address (likely 0xEeeeeEeeeEeEeeEeEeEeeEEEeeeeEeeeeeeeEEeE). This was likely redacted by the platform's log scrubber during code generation. In practice, the zero address check on line 4048 catches the most common native ETH case, and the default tokenlist only includes WETH (ERC-20) not plain ETH, so this doesn't cause a runtime bug in normal usage. However, if a tokenlist ever includes the 0xeeee... sentinel for native ETH, isNativeToken() would fail to detect it and would attempt an eth_call balanceOf on a non-contract address, returning null (silently hidden per error handling).

Race condition in updateFromTokenBalance/updateToTokenBalance: These async functions are called with void (fire-and-forget). If a user rapidly changes tokens, a slower RPC response for the old token could resolve after a faster response for the new token, causing the displayed balance to briefly show the wrong token's balance. A generation counter or abort signal would fix this, but given the 30s cache and typical user behavior, this is unlikely to cause visible issues.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The platform's log scrubber appears to redact certain Ethereum addresses during code generation, replacing them with asterisks. This caused NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS to be set to 42 asterisks instead of a real address. Workers generating code containing well-known Ethereum addresses (like 0xEeeeeEeeeEeEeeEeEeEeeEEEeeeeEeeeeeeeEEeE) should be warned about this and instructed to verify address constants in generated code. The same redaction pattern is visible in the tokenlist JSON bridgeInfo.tokenAddress fields.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts:4042 — NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS = '******************************************' instead of 0xEeeeeEeeeEeEeeEeEeEeeEEEeeeeEeeeeeeeEEeE. Also visible in static/tokenlist.json bridgeInfo entries.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No unit tests were added for the balance formatting logic (formatBalance function). The skill explicitly says client-side-only changes use manual verification, but formatBalance is a pure function that could be extracted and tested. This is consistent with the skill's guidance but represents a gap in test coverage for non-trivial formatting logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff tests.added is empty array. Skill says 'For client-side-only changes: Testing is done via manual verification'. formatBalance has decimal parsing, BigInt arithmetic, and regex-based comma formatting.

summary: Feature token-balance-display passes review. The implementation at commit 50245cb correctly adds wallet token balance display for both from/to token fields with all required behaviors: balance fetching via eth_getBalance/eth_call, 30-second cache, triggers on wallet/token/chain changes, silent error handling, and formatted display with monospace styling. Two non-blocking issues identified: (1) NATIVE_TOKEN_ADDRESS contains redacted asterisks instead of the real 0xeeee... sentinel address — benign in practice since the default tokenlist doesn't include plain ETH; (2) minor race condition potential in rapid token switching. The worker followed the skill procedure and ran all verification commands successfully.
